title: Tephroseris taitoensis
intro: Tephroseris taitoensis, also known as Taito mountain everlasting or alpine everlasting, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a possible treatment for various ailments such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris taitoensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Tephroseris taitoensis is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Within the family Asteraceae, Tephroseris taitoensis is also classified in the tribe Senecioneae, which includes many species of daisies and related plants. The genus Tephroseris is further classified in the subfamily Senecioinae. Tephroseris taitoensis is native to Japan.
uses: Tephroseris taitoensis, a species of flowering plant, has been found to have medicinal properties, although limited research has been conducted on its uses.
